dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9252 IH: Promoting Oversight With Energy Resilience for Moldova Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Ross (for herself and Mr. Lawler) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Foreign Affairs

form.official-title: To require the Secretary of State to submit a report on energy in Moldova.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Promoting Oversight With Energy Resilience for Moldova Act or the POWER Moldova Act of 2026.

section.header: Report to Congress on energy in Moldova

subsection.text: It is the sense of Congress that—

paragraph.text: the United States and Moldova have maintained a strategic bilateral relationship for over 30 years; and

this relationship has deepened in recent years amid Russia’s war of aggression against Ukraine, with both sides prioritizing Moldova’s defense and security cooperation, and resilience against hybrid threats.

subsection.text: Not later than 180 days after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Secretary of State, in coordination with the Secretary of Energy, shall submit to Congress a report on—

paragraph.text: energy production, imports, and consumption in the Republic of Moldova;
the transparency of energy pricing and billing practices in Moldova;
vulnerabilities in energy infrastructure in Moldova;
ongoing international cooperation efforts with Moldova regarding—

subparagraph.text: fostering a better understanding of the United States;
establishing a rapport between the United States Armed Forces and Moldova’s military to build partnerships for the future;
enhancement of interoperability and capabilities for joint operations; and
focusing on professional military education, civilian control of the military, and protection of human rights;

paragraph.text: the potential for renewable energy development in Moldova, including the development of geothermal energy, solar energy, and wind resources; and
an assessment of the feasibility, investment needs, and barriers to deployment with respect to such renewable energy development.
